title: Electromagnetic couplers with multi-band filtering
abstract: A filtered electromagnetic coupler includes a main transmission line extending between an input port and an output port, and a coupled line section extending between a coupled port and an isolation port. The coupler is configured to couple signal power from the main transmission line to provide coupled signals at the coupled port, and a filter subsystem is connected to the coupled port to filter the coupled signals. The filter subsystem includes filters configured to pass or reject coupled signals by frequency, and the filter subsystem provides the filtered output signal to a measurement node.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: CLAIMS 1. A filtered electromagnetic coupler comprising: a main transmission line extending between an input port and an output port; a coupled line section extending between a coupled port and an isolation port and configured to couple signal power from the main transmission line to provide a plurality of coupled signals at the coupled port, each of the plurality of coupled signals being associated with a different frequency band; and a filter subsystem including a plurality of filters and being connected to the coupled port and configured to receive and filter the plurality of coupled signals to provide at least one filtered output signal at a measurement node, the plurality of filters including a first filter configured to pass a first coupled signal of the plurality of coupled signals associated with a first frequency band and to reject a second coupled signal of the plurality of coupled signals associated with a second frequency band, and a second filter configured to pass the second coupled signal and reject the first coupled signal. 2. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of claim 1 further comprising a mode switch operable to selectively provide the plurality of coupled signals as a forward signal representative of a signal traveling from the input port to the output port, or to selectively provide the plurality of coupled signals as a reverse signal representative of a signal traveling from the output port to the input port. 3. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-2 wherein one or more of the plurality of filters is selectively connected between the input port and the measurement node. 4. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-3 wherein each of the plurality of filters is one of a lowpass filter, a bandpass filter, a band-reject filter, and a highpass filter. 5. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-4 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters is an acoustic wave filter. 6. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-5 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters is an integrated passive device filter. 7. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-6 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters is adjustable. 8. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-7 further comprising an adjustable termination impedance circuit connected to the isolation port. 9. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-8 further comprising a load selectively connected to at least one of the plurality of filters. 10. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of claim 9 wherein the load is an adjustable load. 11. A coupler module comprising the filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1- 10. 12. A system comprising: the filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-10; a transceiver configured to produce a transmit signal; and an antenna switch module configured to connect the transceiver to the filtered electromagnetic coupler and to direct the transmit signal to the input port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 13. The system of claim 12 further comprising a power amplifier module connected between the transceiver and the antenna switch module, the power amplifier module being configured to receive and amplify the transmit signal. 14. The system of one of claims 12 and 13 further comprising an antenna connected to the output port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler, the antenna being configured to transmit the transmit signal and to receive a receive signal. 15. The system of claim 14 wherein the filtered electromagnetic coupler is further configured to receive the receive signal at the output port and to provide the receive signal at the input port, and the antenna switch module is further configured to direct the receive signal to the transceiver. 16. The system of any one of claims 12-15 wherein the system is a wireless device. 17. The system of claim 16 wherein the wireless device further comprises at least one processor, a computer readable medium, a controller, and a battery. 18. A module comprising: the filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-10; and an antenna switch module connected to one of the input port and the output port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 19. The module of claim 18 further comprising a power amplifier connected to the antenna switch module, the antenna switch module being connected between the power amplifier and the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 20. A wireless device comprising: the module of any one of claims 11, 18, and 19; and a transceiver configured to produce a transmit signal and to receive a receive signal. 21. The wireless device of claim 20 further comprising an antenna coupled to the filtered electromagnetic coupler and configured to transmit the transmit signal and to receive the receive signal. 22. The wireless device of claim 21 further comprising at least one of a battery, a computer readable medium, a controller, and at least one processor. 23. A method of providing an electromagnetic signal comprising: providing a plurality of coupled signals at a coupled port of an electromagnetic coupler, each coupled signal of the plurality of coupled signals being associated with a different respective frequency band; directing the plurality of coupled signals to at least one of a plurality of filters, the plurality of filters including a first filter configured to pass a first coupled signal of the plurality of coupled signals and to reject a second coupled signal of the plurality of coupled signals, and a second filter configured to pass the second coupled signal and reject the first coupled signal; and providing the first coupled signal to a measurement node. 24. The method of claim 23 further comprising selectively providing the second coupled signal to the measurement node. 25. The method of claim 23 further comprising detecting a power of the first coupled signal at the measurement node. 26. The method of any one of claims 23-25 further comprising detecting the frequency of the first coupled signal. 27. The method of claim 26 further comprising adjusting an adjustable termination impedance connected to an isolation port of the electromagnetic coupler based in part upon the detected frequency of the first coupled signal. 28. The method of claim 26 further comprising adjusting the at least one of the plurality of filters based in part upon the detected frequency of the first coupled signal. 29. A filtered electromagnetic coupler comprising: an input port, an output port, a coupled port, and an isolation port; 

 a measurement node; and a plurality of filters selectively connected between the coupled port and the measurement node, the plurality of filters including a first filter having a first filter characteristic that passes a first coupled signal having a first frequency band and rejects a second coupled signal having a second frequency band, and a second filter having a second filter characteristic that passes the second coupled signal and rejects the first coupled signal, to provide at least one filtered output signal at the measurement node. 30. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of claim 29 wherein the measurement node includes a plurality of measurement nodes and is configured such that the selective connectivity occurs by connecting a measurement device to one of the measurement node nodes. 31. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of one of claims 29 and 30 wherein each of the plurality of filters is one of a lowpass filter, a bandpass filter, a band-reject filter, and a highpass filter. 32. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-31 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters is an acoustic wave filter. 33. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-32 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters is an integrated passive device filter. 34. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-33 wherein at least one of the plurality of filters has an adjustable filter characteristic. 35. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-34 further comprising a load selectively connected to at least one of the plurality of filters. 36. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of claim 35 wherein the load is adjustable. 37. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-36 further comprising a termination impedance connected to the isolation port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 38. The filtered electromagnetic coupler of claim 37 wherein the termination impedance is adjustable. 39. A coupler module comprising the filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 29-38. 40. A wireless device comprising: the coupler module of claim 39; an antenna connected to the output port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler; and a transceiver connected to the input port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 41. The wireless device of claim 40 wherein the transceiver is configured to provide an input signal to the input port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler, the first coupled signal and the second coupled signal being derived from the input signal. 42. A coupler module comprising: the filtered electromagnetic coupler of any one of claims 1-10 or claims 29-38; a communication interface; and a control circuit coupled to the communication interface and coupled to at least one configurable component of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 43. The coupler module of claim 42 wherein the control circuit is configured to receive at least one instruction via the communication interface and configured to select a selectable state of the at least one configurable component. 44. The coupler module of any one of claims 42-43 wherein the configurable component is at least one of a mode selection switch, an adjustable termination impedance, an adjustable filter, a selectable load, and a filter selection switch. 45. The coupler module of any one of claims 42-44 wherein the control circuit is configured to select a coupler state and a filter state to cooperatively provide a desired filtered coupled output at the measurement node. 46. The coupler module of claim 45 wherein selecting a filter state includes selecting a selectable state of at least one of an adjustable filter, a selectable load, and a filter selection switch. 47. The coupler module of any one of claims 45-46 wherein selecting a coupler state includes selecting a selectable state of at least one of a mode selection switch and an adjustable termination impedance. 48. A wireless device comprising: the coupler module of any one of claims 42-47; an antenna connected to the output port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler; and a transceiver connected to the input port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler. 49. The wireless device of claim 48 wherein the transceiver is configured to provide an input signal to the input port of the filtered electromagnetic coupler, the first coupled signal and the second coupled signal being derived from the input signal.
